talk_metadata.talk_title: Immune-stimulating ADCs: payload design, Fc silencing, and dual payload strategies
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we see improved efficacy, so it's a matched dose. This is 10 MPK Q4d times three. So you see the improved efficacy"
Speaker said: "we believe that this is driven by the Fc gamma activity"
Speaker said: "we do not see any meaningful activity on body weight, movement, lethargy"
Speaker said: "we did not do any sort of necroscopy here, but for what it's worth, in life findings are very, very nice here"
Speaker said: "we have not done PK on the payload itself yet"

vulnerability_identified.failing_compound_or_target: First‑generation ISACs (e.g., Bolt, Novartis, Silverback, Mersana, Takeda)
vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Fc gamma‑mediated uptake causing cytokine release syndrome and anti‑drug antibodies
vulnerability_identified.hidden_tox_signal: Speaker noted significant CRS‑related toxicities in early ISACs and ADA formation

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Broad STING expression in non‑tumor tissues dilutes tumor delivery

trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: TLR7 agonist delivered via Fc‑active ADC
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Using Fc‑active antibodies leads to off‑target Fcγ uptake and CRS, diluting tumor‑specific signal
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: No patient selection based on tumor TLR7 expression or immune infiltrate

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker highlighted reliance on Fcγ activity and non‑cleavable linkers, which are known to cause CRS and ADA
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Broad, non‑selected patient population and lack of biomarker‑driven dosing

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that Fc‑active ISACs cause strong cytokine release in PBMC assays, then concluded that Fc silencing will reduce CRS risk – contradictory because cytokine release was attributed to Fcγ uptake, yet Fc silencing also reduces payload delivery to immune cells

key_data_gaps: No OS or long‑term survival data

crispro_opportunity.description: Lack of patient selection based on tumor TLR7 expression or immune infiltrate suggests an opportunity for CrisPRO to develop predictive biomarkers for ISAC response.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted that "Fc gamma‑mediated uptake" drives cytokine release and that "most of the ADC actually ends up in non‑tumor tissue".
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate transcriptomic and spatial immune profiling to identify tumors with high TLR7‑expressing APCs and low peripheral FcγR activity, enabling enriched enrollment.

crispro_opportunity.description: Dual payload ADCs combining a TLR7 agonist with a tubulin inhibitor (MMAF) show synergistic activity without overlapping toxicities, suggesting a platform for CrisPRO to prioritize non‑overlapping payload combos.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker stated: "The toxicities will not necessarily be building on one another" and presented data where the dual payload outperformed single agents.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model pharmacodynamic interactions between immune‑stimulating and cytotoxic payloads to predict optimal DAR ratios and dosing schedules.

cited_competitors.name: Silverback
cited_competitors.context: First‑generation ISAC developer with noted failures

unresolved_questions: What is the optimal DAR ratio for dual‑payload ISACs to balance efficacy and CRS risk?
Can Fc‑silenced ISACs achieve sufficient tumor uptake in patients without FcγR‑mediated internalization?

assets_to_track: TLR7 agonist ISAC programs at Bolt, Novartis, Silverback
Dual‑payload ADCs combining MMAF and immune agonists

talk_metadata.talk_title: PI3K/mTOR inhibitor payloads for ADCs: from hematologic to solid tumors
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "We rationalize by leveraging the tumor‑specific mechanism, we can reduce, enable rational combination, and also treat new indications"
Speaker said: "Our ADC demonstrated tumor regression at 3 MPK, which is impressive"
Speaker said: "We saw no body weight loss associated with any of the ADC group"
Speaker said: "The ADC groups had no body weight loss, whereas alpelisib showed weight loss"
Speaker said: "We observed a threshold effect: payload level above ~100 ng/mL associated with robust p‑S6 suppression"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: On‑target PI3K/mTOR inhibition leads to class‑wide metabolic toxicities (e.g., hyperglycemia) not fully mitigated by ADC delivery
vulnerability_identified.hidden_tox_signal: Speaker noted “target organs are very consistent with approved PI3K inhibitors” implying similar toxicity profile

trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: ADC delivering pan‑PI3K/mTOR inhibitor to CD70b‑positive B‑cell malignancies
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: No biomarker stratification beyond CD70b expression; does not account for PI3K pathway mutations that may affect response
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: PIK3CA mutation status or PTEN loss

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker admitted need to lower DAR and improve site‑specific conjugation, indicating current chemistry may be sub‑optimal
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Broad indication strategy without clear biomarker‑driven patient selection could dilute signal

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker presented sub‑nanomolar in‑vitro potency of the PI3K/mTOR payload, then concluded that the ADC can be used broadly across solid tumors without additional biomarker selection – contradictory to the need for PI3K pathway alterations for efficacy

key_data_gaps: Limited long‑term safety data (e.g., hyperglycemia not observed but not fully explored)

crispro_opportunity.description: Absence of PIK3CA mutation or PTEN loss stratification suggests an opportunity for CrisPRO to develop a predictive gene‑expression signature for PI3K/mTOR ADC response.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker highlighted that "PIK3CA alteration landscape" drives sensitivity and that "PI3K wild‑type tumors can still be sensitive" but did not present data.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate tumor genomics and transcriptomics to define a composite PI3K pathway activation score for patient enrichment.

crispro_opportunity.description: Combination of the PI3K/mTOR ADC with venetoclax showed synergistic tumor regression, indicating a platform for CrisPRO to prioritize BCL2 inhibitors as partners.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "The combination of our PI3K ADC with venetoclax showed exceptional tumor suppression".
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model synergistic PD effects and predict optimal dosing schedules for PI3K ADC + BCL2 inhibitor regimens.

cited_competitors.name: alpelisib
cited_competitors.context: Comparator small‑molecule PI3K inhibitor with observed weight loss

cited_competitors.name: trodelvy
cited_competitors.context: Comparator ADC that was inactive in the models

unresolved_questions: What is the therapeutic window of the pan‑PI3K/mTOR ADC in humans compared to small‑molecule inhibitors?
Can lower DAR formats maintain efficacy while reducing potential aggregation?

assets_to_track: Venetoclax combination trials with PI3K inhibitors

talk_metadata.talk_title: Degrader Antibody Conjugates (DACs): targeted protein degraders as next‑gen ADC payloads
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "We hypothesized that this DAC approach have a really strong potential to deliver both improved efficacy and improved tolerability"
Speaker said: "We did not see significant body weight loss or mouse deaths in these models, highlighting the improved therapeutic index"
Speaker said: "Degrader‑06 had a half‑life of only 34 hours, which is pretty short"
Speaker said: "Degrader‑07 shows >200 hours half‑life and improved stability"
Speaker said: "We see bystander effect in vitro studies"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Rapid plasma hydrolysis of cereblon ligand leading to loss of payload activity
vulnerability_identified.hidden_tox_signal: Speaker noted mouse body weight loss and deaths with the free degrader, indicating systemic toxicity

trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: SMARCA2/4 dual degrader delivered via antibody to PSMA or mutant CALR tumors
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: No selection for tumors with SWI/SNF dependency or SMARCA2/4 expression levels
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: SMARCA2/4 loss‑of‑function or SWI/SNF mutation status

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker disclosed degradation potency (370 pM) and linker chemistry, indicating the core scaffold is the key IP element
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Reliance on high DAR (DAR8) may affect manufacturability and increase heterogeneity

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker demonstrated potent in‑vitro degradation (picomolar) yet admitted that the free degrader caused severe weight loss and deaths, while the ADC showed no toxicity – contradictory regarding the intrinsic safety of the payload

key_data_gaps: No in‑human PK/PD or safety data
Stability of degrader payload within the ADC not fully characterized

crispro_opportunity.description: CrisPRO can model the relationship between intracellular degrader concentration, target protein half‑life, and tumor regression to optimize DAR and linker design for DACs.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker highlighted a threshold payload level (~100 ng/mL) correlating with robust p‑S6 suppression for PI3K ADCs, suggesting similar quantitative relationships could be applied to DACs.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO’s kinetic modeling can predict the minimal intracellular degrader exposure needed for durable target knock‑down, guiding payload optimization.

crispro_opportunity.description: Lack of patient selection based on SWI/SNF complex dependency offers an opportunity for CrisPRO to develop a transcriptional signature to identify tumors most likely to respond to SMARCA2/4 DACs.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted sensitivity in prostate, breast, AML, and multiple myeloma cell lines but did not present a biomarker framework.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate CRISPR‑screen and expression data to derive a predictive score for SMARCA2/4 dependency.

cited_competitors.name: MMAE ADCs
cited_competitors.context: Standard cytotoxic payloads used as benchmark

cited_competitors.name: Topoisomerase I inhibitor ADCs
cited_competitors.context: Standard cytotoxic payloads used as benchmark

unresolved_questions: What is the minimal degradation percentage of SMARCA2/4 required for tumor regression in vivo?
Can the improved plasma stability of Degrader‑07 be maintained after conjugation to different antibodies?

assets_to_track: SMARCA2/4 degrader payload programs at Prelude Therapeutics
DAC platforms from other companies (e.g., Daiichi Sankyo, Pfizer)
